heroHeading: Beautiful Modular Extensions for Manchester Homes
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Manchester | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Manchester homes — kitchens, garden offices and more, built in Blackburn and installed in under a week.
intro:
Manchester is a city of homes people genuinely love — the red-brick terraces of Levenshulme, the bay-fronted semis of Chorlton, the leafy Victorian streets of Didsbury. What most of them share is a familiar problem: not quite enough space. A kitchen that can't fit a dining table, a "spare room" that's really a desk wedged behind a bed, a garden that could be doing far more.

EazyBase builds modular home extensions in Manchester a different way. Your extension is designed around your home, then built in our Blackburn factory — often in as little as four weeks — before being delivered and installed on site in under a week. Less mud, less scaffolding, far fewer months of builders in your kitchen.

Whether you're after a bigger kitchen-diner, a proper garden office or a playroom that keeps the Lego out of the lounge, we handle the whole journey — design, build and installation — from just up the road.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Manchester terrace
localAngles.body: The red-brick terraces of Levenshulme, Chorlton and Didsbury were built to last, but they weren't built for modern life — narrow kitchens, single living rooms and long, underused gardens. A rear or garden extension can transform how these houses work without losing what makes them special. Because our modules are precision-built in the factory, they arrive ready to sit neatly against period brickwork, with clean lines and proper insulation rather than a bolted-on afterthought. And with most of the construction happening off site, your neighbours on both sides will barely notice the build.

localAngles.heading: Garden offices for hybrid workers
localAngles.body: Manchester has one of the UK's liveliest professional scenes, and plenty of its workforce now splits the week between home and the office. If home is a two- or three-bed terrace, a permanent desk is hard to come by — which is why garden offices have become one of our most requested builds across south Manchester. A fully insulated, properly wired garden room gives you a real workspace with a front door of its own, thirty seconds' commute away, and hands the spare bedroom back to being a bedroom.

localAngles.heading: Planning permission in Manchester
localAngles.body: Many of our projects fall within permitted development rights, which often cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms used incidentally to the home — but the rules have limits on size, height and position, so always check with Manchester City Council before you commit. Parts of south Manchester, including areas of Didsbury and Chorlton, sit within conservation areas where extra restrictions can apply. If you're in the Trafford, Salford or Stockport catchment, your own council is the one to ask. We design with these rules in mind and can guide you through what's likely to be needed.

localAngles.heading: Why extend rather than move?
localAngles.body: Moving in Manchester means estate agent fees, legal costs, stamp duty and the very real risk of pricing yourself out of the neighbourhood you chose for a reason — the school catchment, the tram stop, the coffee shop on the corner. Most EazyBase extensions cost between £20,000 and £60,000, delivered and installed, which is why so many homeowners conclude that adding the room they're missing makes more sense than buying it somewhere else. You keep the house you love; we build the space it's missing.

faqs.q: How long would an extension take for a home in Manchester?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory, typically in as little as four weeks, while your garden stays untouched. Installation on site then takes under a week. Blackburn is only a short run down the motorway from Manchester, which keeps delivery simple and lets our team stay closely involved from survey to handover.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Manchester?
faqs.a: Often not — permitted development rights frequently cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms, subject to limits on size and height. If your home is in a conservation area, which includes parts of Didsbury and Chorlton, different rules can apply. Always confirm with Manchester City Council — or Trafford, Salford or Stockport council if you're in their area — and we'll help you understand what your project is likely to need.

faqs.q: My street is a tight terrace with on-street parking. Can you still deliver?
faqs.a: Almost always, yes. We survey access before anything is built and design the modules and delivery method around your street — including narrow terraced roads common in Levenshulme and Chorlton. Because installation takes under a week, any disruption to parking and neighbours is brief, and we plan deliveries and arrange any permits needed in advance.

faqs.q: What do Manchester homeowners typically use their extensions for?
faqs.a: Bigger kitchen-diners are the most popular choice for terraced and semi-detached homes, closely followed by garden offices for hybrid workers. Playrooms, dining rooms and multi-purpose garden rooms are common too. Every EazyBase extension is designed around how you actually want to live, so the same space can flex as your household changes.
